title: [lichen_l2.c:298-307] peer_find_locked iterates full table even after finding match
description:
Gap found: Peer table search efficiency not analyzed.

Why this was missed: Focus was on peer_try_all_pubkeys, not peer_find_locked.

The actual bug: In peer_find_locked() (lines 298-307), the loop iterates through all CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS entries. It returns immediately when a match is found (line 303), so this is actually efficient - it short-circuits on first match.

Wait, reviewing more carefully: the loop structure is correct. The return inside the if statement causes early exit. This is NOT a bug.

However, the related function peer_try_all_pubkeys (already flagged in Round 1) has the same pattern but with expensive signature verification. peer_find_locked is O(n) memcmp which is fast. 

Closing this as a non-issue after review.
close_reason: Not a bug: peer_find_locked already has early return at line 393. The function returns &peer_table[i] immediately upon finding a match and does NOT continue iterating. The line numbers in the issue (298-307) are incorrect - they point to BUILD_ASSERT statements, not the function. This is a false positive.
